notes: Smallest possible round-trip: one user prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc with TotalTokens > 0 after the turn. Recorded with the HEADLESS pi driver (`pi --print -p <prompt>`): pi writes one JSONL session file per invocation on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy), runs the single turn, then exits. The headless turn is brief, so the curated fixture is a SINGLE rollout session (no separately-matchable proc-<PID> pre-session phase — the daemon's first scanner tick already keys the real UUID transcript). The daemon observes that one session born ready, the working flip on first transcript activity, the working→ready settle when the turn completes (pi's stopReason=stop maps to turn_done), then pid_discovered and process_exited at headless exit. turn_end anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready. The TotalTokens > 0 assertion the scenario validates isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL); it's covered by the pi parser unit tests.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Headless pi exits after the single turn — process_exited on the session
